cards.title: PRX Pre-Training Data Strategy: Lance for Curation, MDS for Streaming, Long VLM Captions
cards.why_it_matters: Understanding how a 7B image-generation model's training corpus is assembled—especially the philosophy around caption length, light filtering, and format choices—is directly relevant if you design pre-training pipelines.
cards.summary: PRX's pre-training data mixes public and internal datasets, deliberately avoiding heavy aesthetic filtering to maximize diversity and concept coverage. Images are re-captioned with a VLM using long, faithful captions so that 'noisy' content (logos, screenshots) becomes controllable via prompting rather than being discarded. Lance (columnar, predicate-pushdown) handles curation and feature engineering; Mosaic MDS handles distributed streaming training from object storage.

cards.title: LeRobot v0.6.0 Ships World Model Policies, Six New VLAs, and a Unified Eval CLI
cards.why_it_matters: If you work on robot learning, this release directly affects your toolchain: world model policies (VLA-JEPA, FastWAM, LingBot-VA), GR00T N1.7 integration, reward model APIs, and a unified benchmark CLI are all production-ready now.
cards.summary: LeRobot v0.6.0 adds three world-model policies—VLA-JEPA (latent-space future prediction with zero inference overhead), LingBot-VA (autoregressive video-action with grounded rollouts), and FastWAM (video-generation expert paired with compact action expert). New VLAs include GR00T N1.7 (Cosmos-Reason2-2B backbone), MolmoAct2, EO-1, EVO1, and Multitask DiT. Reward model API, six new simulation benchmarks under lerobot-eval, DAgger-style human-in-the-loop CLI, FSDP training, and 2x faster data loading also ship.

cards.title: CamVLA: Calibration-Free VLA Policy That Estimates Its Own Camera Geometry
cards.why_it_matters: Camera recalibration is a major deployment headache in real-world robotics; a policy that self-computes camera-to-base geometry from a single monocular RGB image could meaningfully simplify robot deployment pipelines.
cards.summary: CamVLA decouples manipulation control from camera geometry by jointly predicting a camera-centric end-effector action (in the local camera frame) and a 6-DoF hand-eye matrix. A deterministic geometric transform composes both into a robot base-frame action. The policy requires only a single monocular RGB image—no depth, no provided extrinsics—and shows improved success rates across diverse unseen viewpoints in simulation and real-world evaluations.

cards.title: Direct-OPD: Transfer RL Policy Shifts from Weak to Strong Models as Implicit Rewards
cards.why_it_matters: Running RLVR on large models is increasingly expensive; this paper offers a concrete, fast alternative—run RL on a small model, then distill the policy *shift* rather than the policy itself into a stronger model.
cards.summary: Direct On-Policy Distillation (Direct-OPD) avoids re-running RL on large models by treating the log-ratio between a post-RL weak teacher and its pre-RL reference as a dense implicit reward for a stronger student. Applied on the student's own on-policy states, no explicit reward model is needed. It boosts Qwen3-1.7B from 48.3% to 62.4% on AIME 2024 in 4 hours on 8 A100s, outperforming step-matched direct RL and supporting sequential composition of multiple policy shifts.

cards.title: LLM-as-a-Verifier: Continuous Logit-Based Scoring Scales Verification Accuracy
cards.why_it_matters: Verification quality directly gates agentic system performance and RL reward density; this training-free framework reaching SOTA on SWE-Bench Verified (78.2%) and Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%) is immediately applicable to agent pipelines.
cards.summary: LLM-as-a-Verifier replaces discrete judge scores with continuous scores computed as the expectation over scoring-token logit distributions. Verification scales along three axes: score granularity, repeated evaluation, and criteria decomposition. It achieves 86.5% on Terminal-Bench V2, 78.2% on SWE-Bench Verified, 87.4% on RoboRewardBench, and 73.3% on MedAgentBench. The framework also provides dense RL feedback, improving SAC and GRPO sample efficiency, and ships an extension for monitoring Claude Code agentic pipelines.

cards.title: Injection-Driven Real-Bogus Classification for Astronomical Surveys Without Human Labels
cards.why_it_matters: Weakly supervised co-teaching with calibrated uncertainty is a transferable technique for any high-throughput detection pipeline, though the domain here is astronomical transient classification rather than ML systems directly.
cards.summary: A dual-network model trained with asymmetric co-teaching classifies real vs. bogus transient candidates using simulated injections instead of human labels, tolerating strong class contamination. A hybrid MC-dropout/dual-network UQ strategy achieves calibrated uncertainty competitive with more expensive deep ensembles. Latent-space analyses show uncertainty concentrates near decision boundaries and reveal substructure in the bogus class. The pipeline is designed for transfer to new surveys via re-running injection-based training.

cards.title: Logical Spectroscopy: Explicit Addressable Logical Bases for Abelian Lifted-Product qLDPC Codes
cards.why_it_matters: Abelian lifted-product codes offer better encoding rates than hypergraph-product codes but lack the explicit logical-operator structure needed for fault-tolerant operations; this construction closes that gap.
cards.summary: Logical spectroscopy uses the Chinese Remainder Theorem to decompose an Abelian lift group's group algebra into Frobenius character packets. Within each packet, kernels, quotients, and product-complex homology are computed over finite fields; CRT idempotents lift representatives back and pair X/Z logicals via trace-dual packets. This yields complete, addressable conjugate logical bases for LP(A,B) codes, certified basis-width upper bounds, and packet-attributed erasure diagnostics—giving Abelian lifted-product families an HGP-like feature.
